description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who keeps a local working copy of a
    remote dataset.  
    A fresh version of that dataset is already staged in the directory

        /home/user/remote_dataset

    and must now be synchronised into your project workspace:

        /home/user/ml_project/data

    The local workspace currently contains an obsolete placeholder file
    (OLD_FILES_DO_NOT_USE.txt) that must be removed during the sync.

    Requirements you must satisfy:

    1. Use a single rsync command (in “archive” mode) that:
       • copies everything found in /home/user/remote_dataset
       • deletes any files in /home/user/ml_project/data that do **not**
         exist in /home/user/remote_dataset  
         (hint: rsync’s “--delete” option)

    2. Capture the complete, on-screen rsync output (both the itemised
       change lines and the final transfer statistics) into the plain-text
       file

        /home/user/ml_project/logs/last_sync.log

       • This file must be overwritten if it already exists.  
       • The log must contain, at minimum, one line for each of the three
         dataset files and one “deleting” line for the obsolete file.
         The automated test will look for lines that match **exactly** the
         four substrings shown below (there may be additional characters
         before each path, supplied by rsync’s item-change flags):

                train.csv
                test.csv
                README.md
                deleting OLD_FILES_DO_NOT_USE.txt

         In addition, the log must end with the usual two rsync summary
         lines that start with:

                sent 
                total size is

    3. After the command finishes:
       • /home/user/ml_project/data must contain exactly three files—
         train.csv, test.csv and README.md—whose contents are byte-for-byte
         identical to the versions in /home/user/remote_dataset.
       • OLD_FILES_DO_NOT_USE.txt must no longer be present in
         /home/user/ml_project/data.
       • /home/user/ml_project/logs/last_sync.log must exist and be
         readable.

    Your deliverable is the correctly executed rsync command and the
    resulting “last_sync.log” file that meets the format expectations
    described above.
truth:
<!--  Initial filesystem state generated by the task runner  -->

    <!--  Remote source directory and its contents  -->
    Directory: /home/user/remote_dataset
        train.csv  (55 bytes):
            id,text,label
            1,Hello world,0

        test.csv   (32 bytes):
            id,text
            1,Hello sync

        README.md  (79 bytes):
            # Remote Dataset v1
            This dataset contains dummy records for sync test.

    <!--  Local project directory before task starts  -->
    Directory: /home/user/ml_project/data
        OLD_FILES_DO_NOT_USE.txt (27 bytes):
            will be removed on next sync

    Directory: /home/user/ml_project/logs
        (empty)

    <!--  Required final state AFTER the agent finishes  -->
    1. /home/user/ml_project/data contains ONLY:
           train.csv   (exact byte match to remote version)
           test.csv    (exact byte match to remote version)
           README.md   (exact byte match to remote version)

       OLD_FILES_DO_NOT_USE.txt must be absent.

    2. /home/user/ml_project/logs/last_sync.log exists and contains:
           • at least one line that ends in “train.csv”
           • at least one line that ends in “test.csv”
           • at least one line that ends in “README.md”
           • a line that reads exactly “deleting OLD_FILES_DO_NOT_USE.txt”
           • summary lines whose prefixes are “sent ” and “total size is”

    The automated checker will verify:
       • checksum equality of the three synced files versus the remote
         originals;
       • non-existence of OLD_FILES_DO_NOT_USE.txt in the destination;
       • presence and readability of the log file with the substrings
         described above.
